title: New Category Handling in Output
description:
When Claude suggests a category not in the existing configuration, I want that category added to the XLSX output with a default color, so that dropdown validation remains correct and no data is lost

Acceptance Criteria:
- Categories returned by Claude that are not in config/categories.yaml are identified as new
- New categories are added to the Lists sheet in the output XLSX
- New categories are assigned a default color in the workbook
- Dropdown validation in the Final Category column includes both predefined and new categories
- The Predicted Category column correctly displays new category values

Topics of Concern:
- New Category Handling: Detecting, storing, and incorporating Claude-suggested categories that are not in the predefined list

Notes: Decision on persistence of new categories (in-memory only vs. written to a config file) is TBD. At minimum, new categories must appear in the output XLSX for the current run.

title: Classification Configuration
description:
When I need to customize how inquiries are categorized, I want to understand the configuration system, so that I can tailor classification rules to my business needs

Acceptance Criteria:
- Documentation explains where configuration files live and the default config path resolution
- Documentation describes the config file format and how to add or modify classification rules
- Documentation warns that rule order matters and explains why (e.g., Refund request must precede Inquiry about plan/pricing)
- Documentation provides an example of adding a new classification category

Topics of Concern:
- Classification Configuration: How the keyword-based classification rules work, how to edit config files, and rule ordering considerations

Notes: Config default path resolves via 3 .parent calls from config.py to project root config/ directory. Rule ordering is critical due to keyword overlap (e.g., 'subscription').

title: Output Format Guide
description:
When reviewing analysis results, I want to understand the XLSX output structure, so that I can interpret the results and integrate them into my workflow

Acceptance Criteria:
- Documentation describes the columns present in the output XLSX file
- Documentation explains the color-coding scheme and what each color represents
- Documentation notes that color fills are static and will not auto-update if the user edits Final Category in Excel
- Documentation provides a sample screenshot or textual description of what a typical output looks like

Topics of Concern:
- Output Format: Structure of the generated XLSX file including sheets, columns, color coding, and known limitations

Notes: XLSX color fills are static—this is a known limitation worth calling out prominently.

title: CLI Integration for Classification Mode
description:
When running the tool from the command line, I want to control which classification method is used, so that I can choose between Claude and keyword classification as needed

Acceptance Criteria:
- By default, the CLI attempts Claude classification and falls back to keyword classification
- A --keyword-only flag forces exclusive use of keyword classification regardless of API key availability
- The CLI reports which classification method was used (Claude, keyword, or mixed) in its output or logs
- The existing CLI interface for input/output file paths and other options is unchanged

Topics of Concern:
- CLI Integration: Wiring Claude classification into the CLI entry point with mode selection

title: Environment Variable Configuration
description:
When setting up the project locally, I want environment-based configuration with a documented example, so that I can supply secrets without committing them to source control

Acceptance Criteria:
- A .env.example file exists documenting all required and optional environment variables
- .env is listed in .gitignore and is never committed
- The application loads environment variables from a .env file when present
- ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is the only new required variable for Claude classification

Topics of Concern:
- Environment Configuration: Managing API keys and runtime settings via .env files and environment variables

title: Anthropic API Client Setup
description:
When configuring the application, I want an Anthropic API client initialized with proper credentials, so that the system can make calls to Claude for classification

Acceptance Criteria:
- Application initializes an Anthropic client using the ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable
- Client targets a Sonnet model for classification requests
- Application raises a clear error message when the API key is missing and Claude classification is explicitly requested
- Application gracefully degrades to keyword classification when the API key is absent and no explicit mode is set

Topics of Concern:
- Anthropic Client Setup: Initializing and configuring the Anthropic SDK client with API key and model selection

title: Claude Message Classification
description:
When processing an inbound inquiry, I want Claude to categorize it based on subject and body against proposed categories, so that classification is more accurate than keyword matching

Acceptance Criteria:
- Each inquiry's subject (when present) and message body are sent to Claude in a single prompt
- The prompt includes the full list of categories from config/categories.yaml as proposed options
- Claude returns exactly one category string per inquiry
- Claude may return a category not in the proposed list when none fit well
- The returned category is stored as the Predicted Category for the inquiry
- Classification handles messages with missing or empty subjects without error

Topics of Concern:
- Claude Message Classification: Sending inquiry subject and body to Claude along with the category list and receiving a classification response

Notes: The prompt should instruct Claude to prefer existing categories and only propose new ones when the inquiry clearly does not fit any existing option.

title: Input Format Specification
description:
When preparing data for analysis, I want to know exactly what input formats and schemas are accepted, so that I can provide well-formed data and avoid parsing errors

Acceptance Criteria:
- Documentation describes the expected JSON structure with a sample snippet
- Documentation describes the expected CSV structure including required column headers
- Documentation lists which fields are required vs optional
- Documentation explains what happens when input is malformed or fields are missing

Topics of Concern:
- Input Formats: Supported input file formats (JSON, CSV), their expected schemas, and field requirements

Notes: The tool supports both JSON and CSV input via parser.py.

title: XLSX Workbook Generation
description:
When inquiries are categorized, I want a formatted XLSX workbook output, so that I can quickly review, filter, and hand-edit categorizations in a familiar spreadsheet interface

Acceptance Criteria:
- Output is a valid .xlsx file named by the user or defaulting to inquiries_categorized.xlsx
- Inquiries sheet contains columns in order: Inquiry ID, Source, Received At (YYYY-MM-DD), Received At (Timestamp), From Name, From Email, Subject, Message Body, Predicted Category, Final Category, Notes
- Rows are sorted by Received At (Timestamp) in a consistent order (newest-first or oldest-first)
- Final Category column has data validation drop-down containing all configured categories
- Source column has data validation drop-down containing all source options: Intercom, In-app contact form, Discord, Direct email inquiry, LinkedIn message
- Final Category defaults to the Predicted Category value for each row
- Notes column is blank and editable

Topics of Concern:
- XLSX Workbook Generation: Generating a properly formatted Excel workbook with the Inquiries sheet, data validations, conditional formatting, and styling

Notes: A helper Lists sheet may be used to store category values and color mappings for the data validation references.

title: Category Configuration
description:
When I need to adjust inquiry categories or their visual styling, I want to edit a simple config file, so that categories can be changed without modifying code

Acceptance Criteria:
- Config file defines a list of category names
- Config file maps each category to a hex color value
- Default config includes all specified categories: Issue with login, Inquiry about plan or pricing, Request for data deletion, Wrong email address (not intended for Polymer), Advertising or solicitation, Knowledge base help, Feature help, Migration, Refund request, Other, Unclear
- Program loads categories and colors from the config file at runtime
- Program raises a clear error if the config file is missing or malformed

Topics of Concern:
- Category Configuration: A config file (JSON or YAML) that defines the list of categories and their associated color mappings

Notes: JSON or YAML format is acceptable. The config file should ship with sensible default colors for all default categories.

title: Inquiry Classification
description:
When I have a batch of normalized inquiries, I want each one automatically categorized, so that I can quickly review and approve predictions rather than categorize from scratch

Acceptance Criteria:
- Each inquiry receives a Predicted Category from the configured category list
- The classifier assigns 'Unclear' when it cannot confidently categorize an inquiry
- Classification uses the subject and message_body fields of the normalized record
- All predicted categories are valid entries from the config file's category list
- Classification runs without requiring external API calls or network access (or the approach is clearly documented if an LLM API is used)

Topics of Concern:
- Inquiry Classification: Auto-categorizing each normalized inquiry into one of the configured categories, including assigning Unclear when confidence is low

Notes: The spec input does not prescribe a specific classification approach (rule-based, keyword matching, or LLM-based). A confidence score is explicitly not required. The implementation should document which approach is chosen.

title: Record Normalization
description:
When inquiries arrive in different schemas, I want them normalized into a single internal record format, so that downstream processing (classification, XLSX output) operates on a consistent structure

Acceptance Criteria:
- Intercom inquiries map to normalized record: inquiry_id (string), source='Intercom', from_name from contact_name, from_email from contact_email, subject, message_body from first_message
- In-app contact form inquiries map to normalized record: inquiry_id (string of numeric id), source='In-app contact form', from_email from reply_to_email, subject, message_body from body
- received_at_date is formatted as YYYY-MM-DD for both input types
- received_at_ts for Intercom is derived from the Unix timestamp provided
- received_at_ts for in-app contact form is the YYYY-MM-DD date converted to a Unix timestamp at midnight (00:00:00) in a consistent timezone
- All normalized records have the same set of fields regardless of input source

Topics of Concern:
- Record Normalization: Mapping fields from each supported input format into a unified internal record with consistent field names and date formats

Notes: The from_name field may be empty for in-app contact form inquiries since that format does not include a name field. The timezone for midnight conversion should be documented or configurable.

title: Unit Tests
description:
When I make changes to the program, I want automated tests to verify core functionality, so that regressions are caught before they reach production

Acceptance Criteria:
- Tests verify parsing Intercom JSON into a correct normalized record
- Tests verify parsing in-app contact form JSON into a correct normalized record
- Tests verify received_at_date is YYYY-MM-DD for both Intercom (from Unix timestamp) and in-app contact form (from date string) inputs
- Tests verify received_at_ts is a correct numeric timestamp for both input types
- Tests verify the generated XLSX contains all required columns in the correct order
- Tests verify Final Category and Source columns have data validation (drop-down) applied
- Tests verify category color fills are applied to Predicted Category and Final Category cells
- Tests verify the Inquiries sheet rows are sorted by received_at_ts

Topics of Concern:
- Unit Tests: Pytest-based unit tests covering parsing, normalization, date handling, and XLSX output verification

Notes: Tests should use pytest. XLSX verification can use openpyxl to read the generated file and inspect structure, validations, and formatting.

title: Fallback to Keyword Classification
description:
When Claude classification is unavailable or not desired, I want the system to fall back to keyword-based classification, so that the application always produces categorized output

Acceptance Criteria:
- Keyword classification is used when the ANTHROPIC_API_KEY environment variable is not set
- Keyword classification is used when a Claude API call fails (network error, rate limit, server error)
- Keyword classification is used when the user passes a --keyword-only flag
- Fallback from a failed Claude call is per-message — successfully classified messages retain their Claude result
- The existing classify() logic in classifier.py remains unchanged and functional

Topics of Concern:
- Fallback Keyword Classification: Conditions and behavior for falling back from Claude to the existing keyword/regex classifier

Notes: Fallback granularity (per-message vs. entire-batch) should be considered. Per-message fallback is preferred so partial Claude failures don't discard successful results.

title: Input Ingestion and Parsing
description:
When I have inbound inquiry data in various file formats and JSON shapes, I want the program to accept and parse them uniformly, so that I can process inquiries from different sources without manual reformatting

Acceptance Criteria:
- Program accepts one or more input files specified as command-line arguments
- Program accepts a JSON array piped via stdin or passed as a string argument
- Program correctly parses Intercom JSON shape with fields: id (string), state, subject, first_message (HTML), contact_email, contact_name, created_at (Unix timestamp)
- Program correctly parses in-app contact form JSON shape with fields: id (number), subject, body (plain text), reply_to_email, created_at (YYYY-MM-DD string)
- Program raises a clear error when input cannot be parsed or is missing required fields
- Program handles both single-object and array-of-objects JSON inputs

Topics of Concern:
- Input Ingestion and Parsing: Accepting input as JSON files, CSV files, or a JSON array via stdin/argument, and parsing the two supported JSON shapes (Intercom and in-app contact form)

Notes: CSV support is required but JSON will be the primary input format. HTML in Intercom first_message should be handled (at minimum preserved as-is in message_body).

title: Pre-commit Hook Setup
description:
When I commit code changes, I want unit tests to run automatically, so that broken code is never committed to the repository

Acceptance Criteria:
- A .pre-commit-config.yaml file exists in the repository root
- The pre-commit config includes a local hook that runs pytest
- The hook runs the full unit test suite on each commit attempt
- A failing test prevents the commit from proceeding
- The pre-commit framework (Python pre-commit package) is used to manage the hook

Topics of Concern:
- Pre-commit Hook Setup: Configuring the pre-commit framework to run pytest as a local hook before each commit

Notes: The pre-commit package (pre-commit.com) must be used rather than a raw .git/hooks/pre-commit script.

title: Installation Guide
description:
When setting up the inbound inquiry analyzer for the first time, I want clear installation instructions, so that I can get the tool running without trial and error

Acceptance Criteria:
- Documentation lists required Python version and how to verify it
- Documentation provides the exact install command (pip install -e '.[dev]')
- Documentation includes a verification step the user can run to confirm successful installation
- Documentation notes any platform-specific considerations (e.g., macOS python3 path)

Topics of Concern:
- Installation Guide: Steps to install Python dependencies, set up the environment, and verify the tool is working

Notes: Python 3.11 is required. System python3 may not resolve correctly; /opt/homebrew/bin/python3.11 is the known-good path on macOS.

title: CLI Usage Reference
description:
When I need to analyze inbound inquiries, I want to know the available commands and options, so that I can run the tool correctly for my use case

Acceptance Criteria:
- Documentation lists all available CLI commands and subcommands
- Each flag/option is described with its purpose and default value
- At least one complete usage example is provided for a typical workflow
- Documentation shows how to specify input file path and output file path

Topics of Concern:
- CLI Usage: Command-line interface commands, flags, arguments, and usage examples

title: XLSX Formatting and Styling
description:
When I open the categorized workbook, I want it to be visually clear and easy to scan, so that I can efficiently review large batches of inquiries

Acceptance Criteria:
- Header row is frozen (stays visible when scrolling)
- Auto-filter is enabled on the header row
- Header row has bold text with a readable background fill
- Message Body column has text wrapping enabled and a wider column width than other columns
- All columns have reasonable default widths
- Predicted Category and Final Category cells are color-filled based on the category-to-color mapping from config
- Rows or category cells where Predicted Category or Final Category is 'Unclear' are visually highlighted distinctly

Topics of Concern:
- XLSX Formatting and Styling: Visual formatting of the workbook including header styling, column widths, text wrapping, color coding, and highlighting

Notes: Color fills should be applied per-cell based on the category value, not as conditional formatting rules (since the values are set at generation time for Predicted Category). Final Category color may need conditional formatting or be set to match Predicted Category initially.

title: Implement Claude classification feature set
description: Implement all 6 Claude classification epics: env vars (.env.example, dotenv), api_client.py, claude_classifier.py, orchestrator.py, new category handling in xlsx_writer, and CLI integration with --keyword-only flag
